title: Diplocyatha ciliata
taxonomy: Diplocyatha ciliata is classified as a species of soft coral in the phylum Cnidaria. It belongs to the class Anthozoa and the order Scleractinia. Within the family Fungiidae, it is placed in the subfamily Fungiinae. Diplocyatha ciliata is further classified as a genus within the family Fungiidae. Its classification is significant for understanding its characteristics and behavior in the marine ecosystem.

intro: Diplocyatha ciliata, also known as the "ciliated jellyfish-like" plant, is a species of marine green algae. Traditionally, Diplocyatha ciliata has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and infection, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
